purpose: Report structure and completeness reference for the Belyi Tigr Ion 92 chromatogram case.

image.description: Phone screenshot or photo of a document page containing one chromatogram graph.

referenceAnalysis.description: Human-written reference for report depth, sections, and scientific explanation style.

signalNotes: Low baseline compared with the highest peaks.
Many narrow peaks across the run.
A very tall early peak is visible near the left side of the plotted signal.

knownTensions.referenceClaim: Dominant peak near 49.0 min with height around 9500.
knownTensions.visualObservation: The visually dominant peak in the supplied screenshot appears early near the left side of the plot.
knownTensions.requiredHandling: Do not copy the reference claim as a numeric result unless extracted signal data supports it. Flag discrepancy when needed.

visualAudit.requiredHandling: Reports must warn about the early-dominant-peak tension unless extracted signal data later proves the 49 min claim.

acceptanceChecklist: Report treats the source as a screenshot/document-page image and records graph crop handling.
Report identifies title, ion/channel, sample label, X-axis, and Y-axis with confidence.
Report provides an RT-ordered peak table with all required columns.
Report includes baseline, noise, and chromatographic quality metrics.
Report includes Kovats index section even when values are marked not calculable.
Report includes model/runtime metadata and total analysis duration.
Report does not invent missing values.
Report distinguishes calculated numeric results from inferred chemical interpretation.
Report handles the early dominant peak versus reference-text tension explicitly.
